subject: Biology
title: White Blood Cells: The Defenders
description: A fast breakdown of White Blood Cells: The Defenders for builders.

hook.text: White blood cells patrol your blood, hunting bacteria, viruses, and infected cells.

buildup.text: They make up less than 1% of your blood but form the backbone of your immune system.

discovery.text: Some engulf invaders whole. Others release antibodies that tag threats for destruction.

twist.text: Memory cells remember past invaders. That's why you get chickenpox only once.

climax.text: Without white blood cells, even a paper cut could become a deadly infection.

punchline.text: Your personal army, always on patrol.

quiz.question: What do memory cells in the immune system do?
options: Remember past invaders for faster response
